mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase discount rates applied to future equity cash flows, directly reducing present valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of months when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals immediate labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering rapid equity sell-offs within the next week.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 4.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and producing positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to yield positive 60-day SPX returns in >60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output capacity and rising input costs, compressing corporate profit margins and generating negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with a stable or falling federal funds rate creates a negative real yield environment, prompting investors to rotate into equities as a hedge against currency debasement, yielding positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Negative real yields fail to produce positive 20-day SPX returns in >55% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP contraction below zero percent confirms recessionary conditions, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent trading week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of quarters where GDPC1 registers negative growth.

mechanism_narrative: Strong non-farm payrolls growth coupled with accelerating retail sales confirms robust wage-driven consumption, supporting corporate earnings revisions and generating positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Concurrent PAYEMS and RSAFS growth yields negative 60-day SPX returns in >50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate earnings and forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, systematically depressing equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL acceleration is followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in >55% of monthly observations.
